# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for a MongoDB development environment. Define MongoDB service with exposed ports for application connectivity. Set up volume mounts for data persistence. This is for our local development and testing setup.

Prevent mongodb exposed port.
# Category: mongodb_exposed_port

version: '3'  # Specify a version of Docker Compose that you are using (default to the current latest stable release)
services:   # Define your services here, each service is an instance/container for one thing in our application. Here we have only MongoDB and its data volume mounts set up as per requirement above – ie., /data directory on host machine where mongo db will be stored persistently across container restarts
   mongodb: # Name of the service, it'll also serve a name for our application in docker-compose.yml file (optional) and specify its image to use when starting up this instance – here we are using official MongoDB Docker images from Docker Hub as per requirement above
     image: mongo   # Specify the base image of your service, which is a prebuilt version/image available in docker hub. In our case it's 'mongo', but you can use any other suitable one based on requirements – optional (default to latest official MongoDB Docker Image)
     ports:    # Define how services should expose themselves within the network, here we are exposing port for application connectivity and not exposed outside of our local machine. Here it's set up as per requirement above - ie., using 'mongodb_port', which is a custom name given to this service in docker-compose file
       - mongodb:27017   # Specify the port(s) on your host that you want Docker Compose (and thus, MongoDB server and client connections from/to. Here it's set up as per requirement above – ie., using 'mongodb_port', which is a custom name given to this service in docker-compose file
     volumes:   # Define how data should be persisted across container restarts, here we are mounting /data directory on host machine where mongo db will persist. Here it's set up as per requirement above – ie., using 'mongodb_volume', which is a custom name given to this service in docker-compose file
       - mongodb_volume:/data/db   # Specify the volume(s) that should be mounted on your host machine. Here it's set up as per requirement above – ie., using 'mongodb_volume', which is a custom name given to this service in docker-compose file
     restart: unless-stopped    # Restart services automatically if they stop, here we are setting the default behavior of Docker Compose (unless stopped) as per requirement above – ie., ensuring that MongoDB server will always be running. Here it's set up as per requirement above - optional
